bylaws.title: Amend BL 1088 – Off Road Vehicles on Municipal Roads
Minimum Maintenance Standards – Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: Adopts minimum maintenance standards for municipal roads and highways as required by Ontario regulation. Defines plowing timelines, pothole repair schedules, and road surface conditions the township must meet.
ai_key_points: Sets snow clearing timelines after storms
Defines acceptable road surface conditions
Pothole repair timelines by road classification
Protects township from liability when standards are met
Based on Ontario Regulation 366/18

bylaws.title: Amending Maximum Weight on Bridges
bylaws.ai_summary: Amends the maximum weight limits permitted on township bridges based on engineering assessments. Restricts heavy vehicles from crossing bridges that cannot safely support their weight.
ai_key_points: Weight limits posted at each affected bridge
Based on structural engineering assessments
Heavy vehicles must use alternate routes
Fines for exceeding posted weight limits
Protects bridges from structural damage

bylaws.title: Landfill By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Governs the use of the township landfill site, including hours of operation, accepted materials, and tipping fees. Residents must show proof of residency to access the facility.
ai_key_points: Proof of residency required for landfill access
Hazardous waste and certain materials prohibited
Tipping fees apply to construction debris and large loads
Seasonal hours of operation posted at the site
Illegal dumping subject to fines and cleanup costs

bylaws.title: Amending Minimum Maintenance Standards Schedule E
Parking By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes parking regulations on township roads and public areas. Restricts overnight parking during winter months to allow for snow clearing operations and sets time limits in designated areas.
ai_key_points: No overnight parking on roads from November to April
Parking restrictions support winter snow removal
Time-limited parking in designated public areas
Vehicles in violation may be towed at owner expense
Fines set for each parking infraction

bylaws.title: Depositing of Snow on any Highway Or Bridge
Procedural By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes rules and procedures for council meetings, including how agendas are set, how motions are made, public participation rules, and rules of order for elected officials.
ai_key_points: Sets regular meeting schedule for council
Defines rules for public delegations and deputations
Outlines how motions and amendments are handled
Establishes quorum requirements
Governs closed session procedures

bylaws.title: Dog By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Regulates dog ownership in Nipissing Township, including licensing requirements, leash rules, and noise provisions. Establishes penalties for owners of dogs that cause disturbances or pose a threat to public safety.
ai_key_points: All dogs must be licensed annually with the township
Dogs must be leashed when off owner property
Owners liable for damage caused by their dog
Maximum number of dogs per household may apply
Fines for barking complaints and at-large violations

bylaws.title: Nuisance Beaver By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Allows property owners to trap or remove nuisance beavers that are causing flooding or property damage, subject to notification to the township and compliance with provincial wildlife regulations.
ai_key_points: Property owners may remove beavers causing damage
Township must be notified before removal
Must comply with provincial trapping regulations
Beaver dam removal permitted to prevent flooding
Township may assist with removal in some cases

bylaws.title: Code of Conduct
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes a code of conduct for members of council and local boards, setting ethical standards for behaviour, conflicts of interest, and use of public resources.
ai_key_points: Council members must declare conflicts of interest
Prohibits use of office for personal benefit
Sets standards for respectful conduct
Complaints handled by the Integrity Commissioner
Penalties range from reprimand to suspension of pay

bylaws.title: Trailer Licensing By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Requires licensing for trailers used as temporary or seasonal dwellings on private property. Sets conditions for trailer placement including lot size, servicing, and duration limits.
ai_key_points: Trailers on private lots require a township license
Annual license must be renewed
Minimum lot and setback requirements apply
Trailer must meet basic health and safety standards
License can be revoked for non-compliance

bylaws.title: Providing Public Notice under the Municipal Act, 2001
User Fees
bylaws.ai_summary: Sets the schedule of user fees charged by the township for various services including building permits, planning applications, landfill use, facility rentals, and administrative services.
ai_key_points: Building permit fees based on project value
Planning and zoning application fees listed
Landfill tipping fees for non-residential waste
Community centre and facility rental rates
Photocopying, search, and admin fee schedule

resolutions.title: the Council of the Township of Nipissing strongly supports Resolution Number 2026-009 from the Municipality of St
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS the Council of the Township of Nipissing strongly supports Resolution Number 2026-009 from the Municipality of St. Charles regarding Nation Building Improvements to Highways 11 and 17; AND WHEREAS drastic increases in fatalities and serious collisions along Highways 11 and 17 demands serious attention; NOW THEREFORE we urge the Province of Ontario to research, implement and enforce safety programming measures; AND THAT the Federal and Provincial governments work together to bring the 2+1

resolutions.title: the Library Agreement states the levy costs to be apportioned as Powassan 60%, Chisholm 20% and Nipissing 20%

Carried.

resolutions.title: we donate $100 to the Powassan Maple Syrup Festival 2026
resolutions.motion_text: THAT we donate $100 to the Powassan Maple Syrup Festival 2026. Carried.

resolutions.title: Council authorizes the Mayor to sign a Letter of Support for the Powassan & District Union Public Library Ontario Tri...
resolutions.motion_text: THAT Council authorizes the Mayor to sign a Letter of Support for the Powassan & District Union Public Library Ontario Trillium Foundation 2026 Capital Grant Application. Carried.

resolutions.title: we receive the 2025 Mayor and Council Expenses and Remuneration Summary, as required by Section 284 of the Municipal ...
resolutions.motion_text: That we receive the 2025 Mayor and Council Expenses and Remuneration Summary, as required by Section 284 of the Municipal Act, 2001. Carried.

resolutions.title: we receive the correspondence report as presented
resolutions.motion_text: That we receive the correspondence report as presented. Carried.

resolutions.title: the statement of accounts dated: February 14, 25, and 26, 2026
resolutions.motion_text: That the statement of accounts dated: February 14, 25, and 26, 2026. Totaling $90,837.73 be approved. Carried.

resolutions.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 3, 2026
resolutions.motion_text: THAT we pass By-Law No. 2026-09, being a By-Law to confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 3, 2026. Carried.

resolutions.title: the meeting be adjourned
resolutions.motion_text: THAT the meeting be adjourned. Time: 8:27 p.m. Carried.

resolutions.title: the minutes of the regular Council Meeting held March 3, 2026, be adopted as published
resolutions.motion_text: THAT the minutes of the regular Council Meeting held March 3, 2026, be adopted as published. Carried.

resolutions.title: provide for the adoption of the current estimates and tax rates, and to further provide for penalty and interest in defa
resolutions.motion_text: That we pass By-Law Number 2026-10, being a By-Law to provide for the adoption of the current estimates and tax rates, and to further provide for penalty and interest in default payment thereof and for the levy of other charges in 2026. Read a first, second and third time and passed this 17th day of March, 2026. Carried.

resolutions.title: set Tax Ratios for Municipal Purposes for the year 2026
resolutions.motion_text: That we pass By-Law Number 2026-11, being a By-Law to set Tax Ratios for Municipal Purposes for the year 2026. Read a first, second and third time and passed this 17th day of March, 2026. Carried.

resolutions.title: the Township of Nipissing acknowledges the required need of large animal veterinary services in the north
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS the Township of Nipissing acknowledges the required need of large animal veterinary services in the north; NOW THEREFORE Council supports the Township of Papineau-Cameron Resolution Number 2026-50, requesting expanded enrollment capacity at the Ontario Veterinary College for those pursuing certification as large animal veterinarians. Carried.

resolutions.title: Council fully supports the statements provided in a letter from the OPP Detachment Board regarding Victim Crisis Assi...
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS Council fully supports the statements provided in a letter from the OPP Detachment Board regarding Victim Crisis Assistance Ontario agencies and the development of a coordinated Provincial Bail Notification Program; NOW THEREFORE we direct this Resolution of support be circulated to the Powassan Nipissing Callander OPP Detachment Board and FONOM. Carried.

resolutions.title: Northern Ontario has limited health care resources requiring travel, wait times and residents struggling to obtain he...
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS Northern Ontario has limited health care resources requiring travel, wait times and residents struggling to obtain health care; NOW THEREFORE Council supports the City of Greater Sudbury in the request that the Province ensure essential medical laboratory services remain accessible within Northern Ontario. Carried.

resolutions.title: we receive the correspondence report as presented
resolutions.motion_text: That we receive the correspondence report as presented. Carried.

resolutions.title: the Municipality of East Ferris is planning construction of a new fire hall that will serve as a regional fire traini...
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS the Municipality of East Ferris is planning construction of a new fire hall that will serve as a regional fire training centre; NOW THEREFORE Council supports the Municipality of East Ferris in its application to NOHFC for funding assistance to construct the East Ferris Regional Fire Training Centre. Carried.

resolutions.title: Council has received a request to waive tipping fees at the Wolfe Lake Landfill Site for items collected by volunteer...
resolutions.motion_text: WHEREAS Council has received a request to waive tipping fees at the Wolfe Lake Landfill Site for items collected by volunteers cleaning the ice surface of Lake Nipissing; NOW THEREFORE Council waives the Landfill Tipping Fees for Bernie Morin with a Temporary Landfill Card dated March 17 to April 17 for disposal of waste collected during the clean up of Lake Nipissing. Carried.

resolutions.title: the statement of accounts dated: February 28, March 6, and 11, 2026
resolutions.motion_text: That the statement of accounts dated: February 28, March 6, and 11, 2026. Totaling $228,406.20 be approved. Carried.

resolutions.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 17, 2026
resolutions.motion_text: THAT we pass By-Law No. 2026-12, being a By-Law to confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 17, 2026. Carried.

resolutions.title: the meeting be adjourned
resolutions.motion_text: THAT the meeting be adjourned. Time: 7:20 p.m. Carried.
